title: polemonium caeruleum subsp. caeruleum
intro: Polemonium caeruleum subsp. caeruleum, also known as the common Jacob's-ladder or blue Jacob's ladder, is a perennial plant native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antispasmodic, and expectorant properties. The health benefits of Polemonium caeruleum subsp. caeruleum include relieving respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as soothing digestive problems and reducing inflammation. Herbal preparations of the plant are typically made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to support respiratory health and alleviate various ailments.
